key_insight: k-means clustering on fingerprinted atomic configurations selects a smaller, more informative training set than random sampling, enabling accurate interatomic potential fitting with far fewer structures.
engineering_relevance: This can reduce DFT labeling cost and speed up development of classical molecular dynamics potentials for materials simulation, especially when only a compact but representative subset is needed.
method: Build configuration fingerprints from CrystalNN-based local environments and radial distribution functions (RDF).
Use k-means to choose representative configurations from a larger pool for EAM fitting.
Visualize fingerprint similarity with t-SNE to identify overlapping/redundant configurations.
evidence_signal: Using 1,800 Ti configurations, the k-means-selected subsets consistently produced lower errors and lower variability than random selection, with about 30 configurations already sufficient for a good EAM model.
watch_item: The method depends on how well the fingerprint representation captures the relevant physics, so poorly chosen descriptors or clustering settings could miss rare but important configurations.
paper_title: k-Means Clustering in Fingerprint-Based Configuration Selection for Fitting Interatomic Potentials
